description: Chapter 1 — Old quantum physics. A guided auxiliary structure for historical experiments, early quantum hypotheses, atomic models and semi-classical quantization.

topics.title: Why old quantum physics matters
topics.note: Historical map of the classical clues that forced a new description of radiation, atoms and matter.

topics.title: Wave optics as the classical benchmark
topics.note: Young's double-slit experiment, Maxwell's wave picture and the reference point for later wave-particle duality.

topics.title: Hydrogen spectral lines
topics.note: From prisms and discrete colors to the Balmer and Rydberg formulas as empirical fingerprints of atomic structure.

topics.title: Cathode rays and the electron
topics.note: Thomson's charge-to-mass measurement, atomic divisibility and the first subatomic particle picture.

topics.title: Black-body radiation
topics.note: Thermal spectra, the ultraviolet catastrophe and Planck's quantum hypothesis as a turning point.

topics.title: Photoelectric effect
topics.note: Einstein's photon interpretation and the experimental signatures that connect light frequency with emitted electrons.

topics.title: Elementary charge
topics.note: Millikan's oil-drop experiment and the quantization of electric charge.

topics.title: Electron diffraction
topics.note: Matter behaving like a wave: diffraction evidence and its connection with de Broglie's idea.

topics.title: Bohr model: postulates
topics.note: Stationary states, angular momentum quantization and quantum jumps in the early atomic model.

topics.title: Bohr model: hydrogen spectrum
topics.note: Energy levels, orbital radii and how the Bohr model explains Hydrogen spectral series.

topics.title: de Broglie hypothesis
topics.note: The matter-wave proposal and its role as a bridge from old quantum physics to wave mechanics.

topics.title: Semi-classical quantization rule
topics.note: Action integrals, allowed orbits and the logic behind early quantization rules.

topics.title: SCQR examples
topics.note: Harmonic oscillator, infinite well and power-law potentials as compact examples of semi-classical reasoning.

topics.title: Sommerfeld orbits and ellipse geometry
topics.note: A complementary bridge between Bohr-Sommerfeld quantization and geometric properties of elliptical motion.

topics.title: Chapter synthesis
topics.note: A compact conceptual summary of the ideas that prepare the transition to Schrödinger's equation.
